review: Good game, I think the main story of the three paths could have been denser, especially the main one about the Pokémon League. The post-game story and the DLCs are good, I think they could have gone even deeper into the topic of paradoxes, like including them more in the main story, not just in the **** addition, they could have “locked” some areas so that it wouldn't be so easy to break the game. Something I'd like to see in the future, if they decide to keep the world open, is for trainers and gym leaders to have flexible teams based on your medals. That way, you could almost decide your path, with certain limitations, of course, such as giving you the freedom to explore the first four areas and go in whatever order you want for the first four gyms, and so on.Finally, improve the graphics. I'm not asking to be able to see the characters' sweat, but I would like to see better-constructed landscapes, with little or no popping. and something they absolutely must focus on in future games is that areas with wild Pokémon should feel more alive, not just with Pokémon walking or flying around, but doing different things, just like in the anime, with Pokémon interacting with the environment, and each Pokémon with its own nature changing depending on your behavior, as well as other trainers. It would be interesting to go along the routes and see NPCs trying to catch a Pokémon or training.

I believe that this game has better gameplay than sword and shield, but that sword and shield has the superior story. Both, however, pale in comparison to Sun and moon.
I think these games get a bad rep from modern consumers being overly critical and negative without playing the game to enjoy, but to rage at it. The current user score is a good example of people not having reigns over their emotions. P.S. Nemona is a freaking joke
Needs more polish.......................................................... Why are we releasing it with so many bugs and a general lack of quality compared to the other Pokémon games?
please for the love of god don't buy this game its just not worthy, but you can emulate it if you want to give it a try

I have never played a game that lifeless before. Compared to the old DS pokemon games, this does not even feel like playing pokemon anymore. Annoying that you had to update it so often, just for it to somewhat work more or less. Personally, I'm beyond disappointed.

I didn't like it.A very buggy game in terms of graphics.Horrible for A modern game of Pokémon Which saves a **** tera form that can implement some legal strategies Apart from that No  I wouldn't recommend it to anyone.
Definitely the worst pokemon game ever (except bdsp) I would care if it disappeared

This game is an embarrassment for a franchise that once defined handheld RPGs. On the Nintendo Switch — a console that has delivered technical marvels like Breath of the Wild and Super Mario Odyssey — this game looks and runs like a rushed beta.Performance: Constant frame drops, glitches, and visual bugs plague the experience. The open world is riddled with pop-in, broken textures, and laughably poor animations. It feels unfinished, as if Game Freak had no idea how to develop for modern hardware.Graphics: Bland environments, flat lighting, and character models that look outdated even by 3DS standards. The Switch has proven it can handle vast, beautiful worlds, but Scarlet settles for mediocrity.Gameplay: While the open-world concept had potential, it’s wasted on repetitive objectives, shallow exploration, and a lack of meaningful progression. The “freedom” is an illusion — the game funnels you into predictable battles and fetch quests.Story & Characters: Forgettable, uninspired, and padded with dialogue that adds nothing. Rivals and NPCs are caricatures, offering no emotional weight or memorable arcs.Overall Polish: Scarlet feels like a cash grab, banking on the Pokémon brand rather than delivering a product worthy of the Switch era.Verdict: Pokémon Scarlet is the worst kind of disappointment — a flagship title that fails to meet even basic standards of quality. On a console capable of breathtaking experiences, Scarlet stands out as a shameful reminder of how far the series has fallen.

It is decent if you play it on Switch 2, if you don't, it's a disgrace. The story is very fun, the graphics are bad but at least colorful, having an open world pokemon game is great but the world is also kind of empty except for the creatures.
Thanks to the Switch 2, it's now perfectly playable, now you can enjoy how bad it is at 60 FPS. And even then, it doesn't run perfectly in Area 0. What a terrible game.

I always thought I loved the Pokémon franchise, but Pokémon Scarlet showed me that wasn't quite the case. The game starts out interesting, the first steps give the impression that it's going to be something special. But when you compare the "open world" with Pokémon Legends: Arceus, it becomes clear that Scarlet is worse, even being the most **** addition, the game has many bugs and technical problems. Even after patches to fix them, almost nothing was resolved. The biggest problem, however, is the player's freedom. You can explore, but you are not significantly rewarded, which makes the experience **** the end, it seems that the franchise learned nothing from The Legend of Zelda, which showed how to give freedom and reward each choice. Scarlet starts well, but quickly gets lost in technical flaws and design **** one point, I just wanted this suffering to end.
ABSOLUTE PEAK; WHAT IS THAT ZELDA GAME. THIS IS THE MASTERPIECE EVERYONE SHOULD TALK ABOUT
Just when you thought Gamefreak couldn't make a cheaper game than Sword and Shield, they do.

A very hollow and grotesque experience. An amorphous collection of bile masquerading as a piece of entertainment software. It is as if a misshapen lump of digital flesh gained the ability to hate. Despite its clear disdain for the mortal coil along with everything good and holy in this world, it still struggles along, twisting and pulsating to maintain circulation and erratically convulsing past any brief respite of respiratory arrest. It has no mouth yet it must scream.

The technical side is completely outdated—it honestly feels like playing a PS2 game. The level design is awful, with a huge but empty and confusing map. Dungeons are basically nonexistent, just simple corridors. Pokémon are ridiculously small, so you spend more time watching your feet than exploring the world, which you’ll never really remember anyway. The Sierra Napada area is terrible, a real insult to players. Cities are totally empty, and NPCs have nothing interesting to say. The camera control is horrible. Most battles end up with the camera clipping through the ground, walls, or simply missing half of the Pokémon on screen. The lack of voice acting is becoming more and more painful, especially during key story moments. The idea of having three main questlines is nice, but it’s ruined by the lack of automatic level scaling. You can easily end up fighting a gym leader with level 20 Pokémon when your team is already level 50. Gym challenges are extremely dumb—the Psychic-type gym is probably the worst example. Characters are cute and well designed, but there are no side quests or events to interact with them, so you never get attached to anyone like in older games. The teachers are a bit more present since you attend classes and can talk to them between lessons—my favorite is Guaro. Pokémon models look bad; you can literally see the polygons. The new Terastalization mechanic is awful, especially compared to old systems like Gigamax or Mega Evolutions. The game is way too easy and lacks any difficulty settings. The idea of giving you the legendary Pokémon at the start is nice, but it’s not enough to save the experience. The post-game is almost nonexistent—basically locked behind paid DLC. In short: a game with some good ideas, but poorly executed in every way.
Nice gameeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeerrrrrrrrrrrrrrrr

Hoo boy! where do i even begin with this abomination? Graphism Trash! Story? Garbage! Performances? Atrocious! again more than half of the pokemons are missing! guess where? yep again locked behind a paywall DLC! Same **** as always Gamefreak! Again like in Sword & Shield animations are the same! More lies and scummy practices to deceive players! you call that open world? it's not even lvl scaled properly! avoid at any cost! Boycott the game!

The game is.... Pokemon.
I can hardly say any more things about it as it states a lot of things on its own.

Open world looks awful and is mostly empty, it's just so boring.
Added to that, the game is a semi-broken mess.
Performance is awful as is and just by playing the game you'll encounter more bugs that Red and Blue back then in Gen1.

Boring, buggy and overpriced.
A game released in a hurry, the result was a game with a lack of performance, horrible quality and divided into DLCs.
You know what. This is a good game overall just having some technical problems. I suggest getting the Switch 2 version as it’s a much better experience!

This is the Generation 9 Pokemon game, it takes place in Paldea Region which is based on Spain. It's a fun game but it's lacking in content and it's not polished or finished. It is not very professionally made. Some of the pokemon designs are really ugly. I still thought it was fun though and probably worth like $20 honestly lol.

Pokemon Scarlet is the first time in a LONG time that I completed a Pokemon game and the this is the first Pokemon game I have finished the Pokédex!

Before anything else I must say I did play this on the Nintendo Switch 2 and OH MAN does it make a difference! And I am only writing this review now as this was the first time I played it and the experience was so good! 60fps, shiny hunting made a lot easier and the time consuming nonsense that older titles had were no where to be seen (except you Gimighoul Coins!)

So after 80+ hours clocked it is safe to say that I had a great time with Pokemon Scarlet and am looking forward to Gen 10!

But again play this on Nintendo Switch 2 as that is the best way to play and is a shame that it released the way it did at launch.

8/10
Didnt play this until after the Switch2 released. What an amazing game. If this released on S2 hardware the reviews would be very different. One of the best Pokémon experiences I have had.

It's a very interesting game with a good story but the graphics are horrible and it's very uncomfortable to play it with such a graphic like these. But the Plot and the Pokémons of this game are amazing and it makes very fun to play it. The end of the game was also a good and a little bit sad ending and even after you finished the game, you can do a lot of things in the game like buy a DLC or go Shiy Hunting and many more things to do. It has some bad sides but also good sides so it is a very valude game.

Okay, I have to be honest. I'm sending my copy of Pokemon Scarlet and Violet back. As a long-time fan, it's a real ****'s talk about the technical stuff first because you can't ignore it. This game is rough. I'm not just talking about the graphics looking a bit dated, I'm talking about the game slowing down constantly in the open world, especially around the lake. I had Pokemon and trees pop into existence right in front of me all the time. And the bugs... I once got stuck inside a rock and had to fast travel out. Another time, the game just froze during a picnic. It feels less like I'm playing a finished game and more like I'm testing a broken early **** the world itself feels so empty and lazy. Take the big main city, Mesagoza. It's huge, but it's all for show. You can't go into almost any of the buildings. Every street looks the same. Compare that to a place like Goldenrod City from the Gold and Silver days, which felt alive with a department store, a radio tower, the Magnet Train station. Mesagoza is just a pretty backdrop, and a repetitive one at that. The open world is just a bunch of empty fields. Where's the clever level design like the twisty paths of Mt. Coronet from Diamond and Pearl?But what really gets me is how the stories are told. The game has some serious moments, especially with Arven and his sick dog, Mabosstiff. But having these emotional scenes play out with just text boxes and characters staring blankly at each other is just awkward. It completely kills the mood. Games from the Nintendo DS era like Pokemon Black and White had way more compelling stories and characters without needing voice acting, but Scarlet and Violet's dialogue feels flat and forgettable in **** just feels like the developers cut every corner they could. For the most successful media franchise on the planet, this is not good enough. We've seen them make beautiful, polished games with heart on much weaker consoles. Playing this just makes me feel like they don't think we deserve better.

I have a lot of problems with this game, and I believe most of it starts with throwing away the things that sword and shield did right. The art style went from anime style that fit Pokémon to a dull color palette and uncanny semirealism that makes the protagonists look like porcelain dolls. The customization nose dived going from fun outfits with cute skirts, hats, heart pupils and vibrant hair colors that let me express the fairy type trainer I am, to now suddenly losing my individuality with a schooluniform and dozens of helmets. The world also suffers. Most of would is just empty grass lands with nothing noteworthy. No magical mushroom forests, volcanic caverns, desert dunes, ruins, reefs, large rivers. There is also severe Lack of environmental features like bushes, boulders; flowers, tall grass, trees and everything else. Most of the buildings you can’t interact with and half the ones you can you can’t enter and pull up menus instead. Lastly I find team stars story to drag the game down. The message is preachy with unsatisfying conclusion. It doesn’t help that this was the third playground bully team in row. 

A Pokémon game is about going on an adventure and most the aspects that immerse the player were neglected and team star as antagonist did nothing to push our imagination.

This game is wayyyy to over hated,I enjoy this game very much the open world is amazing some of the new pokemon they added are so unique, I don't enjoy the graphics and art style though,I also don't enjoy how they pretty much ditched animations, butI has tons of fun with this game, and have sunk many hours into the game and the dlc.

Pokemon and characters still look lifeless. The routes to each gym/new area don't feel like unique adventures like they used to. Legendaries feel very anticlimactic when you encounter them. The story is godawful and meant for babies. The difficulty is laughable. You have no emotional investment with any of the characters as the plot line is so shallow. Unlimited exp candies is a ridiculously stupid thing to add, it just makes the game way too easy if you dont want to work. Don't even get me started on how bad the textures look and how poorly the game runs. New pokemon designs are uninspired. It used to take TPC a month to design each pokemon, now they just throw slop at a wall and don't even wait to see what sticks. What a disappointment.

Pokémon Scarlet & Violet has fun core gameplay — gym battles and catching Pokémon are as enjoyable as ever — but the experience is dragged down by weak performance and forgettable characters. The open world is a cool idea that just doesn’t live up to its potential, running poorly on the Switch and feeling underwhelming overall.
The game was too buggy to release and I had to suffer through the experience when it came out.  Shame on the lead developers and directors who early shipped this game.

Anyone that shills for these games are NOT a pokemon fan. All of these games are fundamentally different from the core Pokemon experience. If your idea of Pokemon is a truly turn-based RPG where you RANDOMLY encounter pokemon and the focus is on building your team. These games are not for you. This is not Pokemon.

What a disappointment and buggy mess. I am a long time Pokemom fan, since the Gameboy game in 1995, and this game is my last Pokemon game. Legends ZA looks to be more of the same lazy slop. Nintendo owns this billion dollar franchise and hasn't added anything innovative in a decade. Sword and Shield were also mediocre.... Legends Arceus was the only enjoyable Pokemon game on the switch and switch 2.

I didn't like it.A very buggy game in terms of graphics.Horrible for A modern game of Pokémon Which saves a **** tera form that can implement some legal strategies Apart from that No  I wouldn't recommend it to anyone.

This game is an embarrassment for a franchise that once defined handheld RPGs. On the Nintendo Switch — a console that has delivered technical marvels like Breath of the Wild and Super Mario Odyssey — this game looks and runs like a rushed beta.Performance: Constant frame drops, glitches, and visual bugs plague the experience. The open world is riddled with pop-in, broken textures, and laughably poor animations. It feels unfinished, as if Game Freak had no idea how to develop for modern hardware.Graphics: Bland environments, flat lighting, and character models that look outdated even by 3DS standards. The Switch has proven it can handle vast, beautiful worlds, but Scarlet settles for mediocrity.Gameplay: While the open-world concept had potential, it’s wasted on repetitive objectives, shallow exploration, and a lack of meaningful progression. The “freedom” is an illusion — the game funnels you into predictable battles and fetch quests.Story & Characters: Forgettable, uninspired, and padded with dialogue that adds nothing. Rivals and NPCs are caricatures, offering no emotional weight or memorable arcs.Overall Polish: Scarlet feels like a cash grab, banking on the Pokémon brand rather than delivering a product worthy of the Switch era.Verdict: Pokémon Scarlet is the worst kind of disappointment — a flagship title that fails to meet even basic standards of quality. On a console capable of breathtaking experiences, Scarlet stands out as a shameful reminder of how far the series has fallen.

It is decent if you play it on Switch 2, if you don't, it's a disgrace. The story is very fun, the graphics are bad but at least colorful, having an open world pokemon game is great but the world is also kind of empty except for the creatures.
Thanks to the Switch 2, it's now perfectly playable, now you can enjoy how bad it is at 60 FPS. And even then, it doesn't run perfectly in Area 0. What a terrible game.

I always thought I loved the Pokémon franchise, but Pokémon Scarlet showed me that wasn't quite the case. The game starts out interesting, the first steps give the impression that it's going to be something special. But when you compare the "open world" with Pokémon Legends: Arceus, it becomes clear that Scarlet is worse, even being the most **** addition, the game has many bugs and technical problems. Even after patches to fix them, almost nothing was resolved. The biggest problem, however, is the player's freedom. You can explore, but you are not significantly rewarded, which makes the experience **** the end, it seems that the franchise learned nothing from The Legend of Zelda, which showed how to give freedom and reward each choice. Scarlet starts well, but quickly gets lost in technical flaws and design **** one point, I just wanted this suffering to end.
ABSOLUTE PEAK; WHAT IS THAT ZELDA GAME. THIS IS THE MASTERPIECE EVERYONE SHOULD TALK ABOUT
Just when you thought Gamefreak couldn't make a cheaper game than Sword and Shield, they do.

A very hollow and grotesque experience. An amorphous collection of bile masquerading as a piece of entertainment software. It is as if a misshapen lump of digital flesh gained the ability to hate. Despite its clear disdain for the mortal coil along with everything good and holy in this world, it still struggles along, twisting and pulsating to maintain circulation and erratically convulsing past any brief respite of respiratory arrest. It has no mouth yet it must scream.

The technical side is completely outdated—it honestly feels like playing a PS2 game. The level design is awful, with a huge but empty and confusing map. Dungeons are basically nonexistent, just simple corridors. Pokémon are ridiculously small, so you spend more time watching your feet than exploring the world, which you’ll never really remember anyway. The Sierra Napada area is terrible, a real insult to players. Cities are totally empty, and NPCs have nothing interesting to say. The camera control is horrible. Most battles end up with the camera clipping through the ground, walls, or simply missing half of the Pokémon on screen. The lack of voice acting is becoming more and more painful, especially during key story moments. The idea of having three main questlines is nice, but it’s ruined by the lack of automatic level scaling. You can easily end up fighting a gym leader with level 20 Pokémon when your team is already level 50. Gym challenges are extremely dumb—the Psychic-type gym is probably the worst example. Characters are cute and well designed, but there are no side quests or events to interact with them, so you never get attached to anyone like in older games. The teachers are a bit more present since you attend classes and can talk to them between lessons—my favorite is Guaro. Pokémon models look bad; you can literally see the polygons. The new Terastalization mechanic is awful, especially compared to old systems like Gigamax or Mega Evolutions. The game is way too easy and lacks any difficulty settings. The idea of giving you the legendary Pokémon at the start is nice, but it’s not enough to save the experience. The post-game is almost nonexistent—basically locked behind paid DLC. In short: a game with some good ideas, but poorly executed in every way.
Nice gameeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeerrrrrrrrrrrrrrrr

Hoo boy! where do i even begin with this abomination? Graphism Trash! Story? Garbage! Performances? Atrocious! again more than half of the pokemons are missing! guess where? yep again locked behind a paywall DLC! Same **** as always Gamefreak! Again like in Sword & Shield animations are the same! More lies and scummy practices to deceive players! you call that open world? it's not even lvl scaled properly! avoid at any cost! Boycott the game!

The game is.... Pokemon.
I can hardly say any more things about it as it states a lot of things on its own.

Open world looks awful and is mostly empty, it's just so boring.
Added to that, the game is a semi-broken mess.
Performance is awful as is and just by playing the game you'll encounter more bugs that Red and Blue back then in Gen1.

Boring, buggy and overpriced.
A game released in a hurry, the result was a game with a lack of performance, horrible quality and divided into DLCs.
You know what. This is a good game overall just having some technical problems. I suggest getting the Switch 2 version as it’s a much better experience!

This is the Generation 9 Pokemon game, it takes place in Paldea Region which is based on Spain. It's a fun game but it's lacking in content and it's not polished or finished. It is not very professionally made. Some of the pokemon designs are really ugly. I still thought it was fun though and probably worth like $20 honestly lol.

Pokemon Scarlet is the first time in a LONG time that I completed a Pokemon game and the this is the first Pokemon game I have finished the Pokédex!

Before anything else I must say I did play this on the Nintendo Switch 2 and OH MAN does it make a difference! And I am only writing this review now as this was the first time I played it and the experience was so good! 60fps, shiny hunting made a lot easier and the time consuming nonsense that older titles had were no where to be seen (except you Gimighoul Coins!)

So after 80+ hours clocked it is safe to say that I had a great time with Pokemon Scarlet and am looking forward to Gen 10!

But again play this on Nintendo Switch 2 as that is the best way to play and is a shame that it released the way it did at launch.

8/10
Didnt play this until after the Switch2 released. What an amazing game. If this released on S2 hardware the reviews would be very different. One of the best Pokémon experiences I have had.

It's a very interesting game with a good story but the graphics are horrible and it's very uncomfortable to play it with such a graphic like these. But the Plot and the Pokémons of this game are amazing and it makes very fun to play it. The end of the game was also a good and a little bit sad ending and even after you finished the game, you can do a lot of things in the game like buy a DLC or go Shiy Hunting and many more things to do. It has some bad sides but also good sides so it is a very valude game.

Okay, I have to be honest. I'm sending my copy of Pokemon Scarlet and Violet back. As a long-time fan, it's a real ****'s talk about the technical stuff first because you can't ignore it. This game is rough. I'm not just talking about the graphics looking a bit dated, I'm talking about the game slowing down constantly in the open world, especially around the lake. I had Pokemon and trees pop into existence right in front of me all the time. And the bugs... I once got stuck inside a rock and had to fast travel out. Another time, the game just froze during a picnic. It feels less like I'm playing a finished game and more like I'm testing a broken early **** the world itself feels so empty and lazy. Take the big main city, Mesagoza. It's huge, but it's all for show. You can't go into almost any of the buildings. Every street looks the same. Compare that to a place like Goldenrod City from the Gold and Silver days, which felt alive with a department store, a radio tower, the Magnet Train station. Mesagoza is just a pretty backdrop, and a repetitive one at that. The open world is just a bunch of empty fields. Where's the clever level design like the twisty paths of Mt. Coronet from Diamond and Pearl?But what really gets me is how the stories are told. The game has some serious moments, especially with Arven and his sick dog, Mabosstiff. But having these emotional scenes play out with just text boxes and characters staring blankly at each other is just awkward. It completely kills the mood. Games from the Nintendo DS era like Pokemon Black and White had way more compelling stories and characters without needing voice acting, but Scarlet and Violet's dialogue feels flat and forgettable in **** just feels like the developers cut every corner they could. For the most successful media franchise on the planet, this is not good enough. We've seen them make beautiful, polished games with heart on much weaker consoles. Playing this just makes me feel like they don't think we deserve better.

I have a lot of problems with this game, and I believe most of it starts with throwing away the things that sword and shield did right. The art style went from anime style that fit Pokémon to a dull color palette and uncanny semirealism that makes the protagonists look like porcelain dolls. The customization nose dived going from fun outfits with cute skirts, hats, heart pupils and vibrant hair colors that let me express the fairy type trainer I am, to now suddenly losing my individuality with a schooluniform and dozens of helmets. The world also suffers. Most of would is just empty grass lands with nothing noteworthy. No magical mushroom forests, volcanic caverns, desert dunes, ruins, reefs, large rivers. There is also severe Lack of environmental features like bushes, boulders; flowers, tall grass, trees and everything else. Most of the buildings you can’t interact with and half the ones you can you can’t enter and pull up menus instead. Lastly I find team stars story to drag the game down. The message is preachy with unsatisfying conclusion. It doesn’t help that this was the third playground bully team in row. 

A Pokémon game is about going on an adventure and most the aspects that immerse the player were neglected and team star as antagonist did nothing to push our imagination.

This game is wayyyy to over hated,I enjoy this game very much the open world is amazing some of the new pokemon they added are so unique, I don't enjoy the graphics and art style though,I also don't enjoy how they pretty much ditched animations, butI has tons of fun with this game, and have sunk many hours into the game and the dlc.

Pokemon and characters still look lifeless. The routes to each gym/new area don't feel like unique adventures like they used to. Legendaries feel very anticlimactic when you encounter them. The story is godawful and meant for babies. The difficulty is laughable. You have no emotional investment with any of the characters as the plot line is so shallow. Unlimited exp candies is a ridiculously stupid thing to add, it just makes the game way too easy if you dont want to work. Don't even get me started on how bad the textures look and how poorly the game runs. New pokemon designs are uninspired. It used to take TPC a month to design each pokemon, now they just throw slop at a wall and don't even wait to see what sticks. What a disappointment.

Pokémon Scarlet & Violet has fun core gameplay — gym battles and catching Pokémon are as enjoyable as ever — but the experience is dragged down by weak performance and forgettable characters. The open world is a cool idea that just doesn’t live up to its potential, running poorly on the Switch and feeling underwhelming overall.
The game was too buggy to release and I had to suffer through the experience when it came out.  Shame on the lead developers and directors who early shipped this game.

Anyone that shills for these games are NOT a pokemon fan. All of these games are fundamentally different from the core Pokemon experience. If your idea of Pokemon is a truly turn-based RPG where you RANDOMLY encounter pokemon and the focus is on building your team. These games are not for you. This is not Pokemon.

What a disappointment and buggy mess. I am a long time Pokemom fan, since the Gameboy game in 1995, and this game is my last Pokemon game. Legends ZA looks to be more of the same lazy slop. Nintendo owns this billion dollar franchise and hasn't added anything innovative in a decade. Sword and Shield were also mediocre.... Legends Arceus was the only enjoyable Pokemon game on the switch and switch 2.

Good game, I think the main story of the three paths could have been denser, especially the main one about the Pokémon League. The post-game story and the DLCs are good, I think they could have gone even deeper into the topic of paradoxes, like including them more in the main story, not just in the **** addition, they could have “locked” some areas so that it wouldn't be so easy to break the game. Something I'd like to see in the future, if they decide to keep the world open, is for trainers and gym leaders to have flexible teams based on your medals. That way, you could almost decide your path, with certain limitations, of course, such as giving you the freedom to explore the first four areas and go in whatever order you want for the first four gyms, and so on.Finally, improve the graphics. I'm not asking to be able to see the characters' sweat, but I would like to see better-constructed landscapes, with little or no popping. and something they absolutely must focus on in future games is that areas with wild Pokémon should feel more alive, not just with Pokémon walking or flying around, but doing different things, just like in the anime, with Pokémon interacting with the environment, and each Pokémon with its own nature changing depending on your behavior, as well as other trainers. It would be interesting to go along the routes and see NPCs trying to catch a Pokémon or training.

I believe that this game has better gameplay than sword and shield, but that sword and shield has the superior story. Both, however, pale in comparison to Sun and moon.
I think these games get a bad rep from modern consumers being overly critical and negative without playing the game to enjoy, but to rage at it. The current user score is a good example of people not having reigns over their emotions. P.S. Nemona is a freaking joke
Needs more polish.......................................................... Why are we releasing it with so many bugs and a general lack of quality compared to the other Pokémon games?
please for the love of god don't buy this game its just not worthy, but you can emulate it if you want to give it a try

The technical side is completely outdated—it honestly feels like playing a PS2 game. The level design is awful, with a huge but empty and confusing map. I have a lot of problems with this game, and I believe most of it starts with throwing away the things that sword and shield did right. The art style went from anime style that fit Pokémon to a dull color palette and uncanny semirealism that makes the protagonists look like porcelain dolls. The customization nose dived going from fun outfits with cute skirts, hats, heart pupils and vibrant hair colors that let me express the fairy type trainer I am, to now suddenly losing my individuality with a schooluniform and dozens of helmets. This game is wayyyy to over hated,I enjoy this game very much the open world is amazing some of the new pokemon they added are so unique, I don't enjoy the graphics and art style though,I also don't enjoy how they pretty much ditched animations, butI has tons of fun with this game, and have sunk many hours into the game and the dlc.

Pokémon Scarlet & Violet has fun core gameplay — gym battles and catching Pokémon are as enjoyable as ever — but the experience is dragged down by weak performance and forgettable characters. The open world is a cool idea that just doesn’t live up to its potential, running poorly on the Switch and feeling underwhelming overall.

Good game, I think the main story of the three paths could have been denser, especially the main one about the Pokémon League. The post-game story and the DLCs are good, I think they could have gone even deeper into the topic of paradoxes, like including them more in the main story, not just in the **** addition, they could have “locked” some areas so that it wouldn't be so easy to break the game. Something I'd like to see in the future, if they decide to keep the world open, is for trainers and gym leaders to have flexible teams based on your medals. That way, you could almost decide your path, with certain limitations, of course, such as giving you the freedom to explore the first four areas and go in whatever order you want for the first four gyms, and so on.Finally, improve the graphics. I'm not asking to be able to see the characters' sweat, but I would like to see better-constructed landscapes, with little or no popping. and something they absolutely must focus on in future games is that areas with wild Pokémon should feel more alive, not just with Pokémon walking or flying around, but doing different things, just like in the anime, with Pokémon interacting with the environment, and each Pokémon with its own nature changing depending on your behavior, as well as other trainers. It would be interesting to go along the routes and see NPCs trying to catch a Pokémon or training.

I believe that this game has better gameplay than sword and shield, but that sword and shield has the superior story. Both, however, pale in comparison to Sun and moon.
I think these games get a bad rep from modern consumers being overly critical and negative without playing the game to enjoy, but to rage at it. The current user score is a good example of people not having reigns over their emotions. P.S. Nemona is a freaking joke

And I am only writing this review now as this was the first time I played it and the experience was so good! 60fps, shiny hunting made a lot easier and the time consuming nonsense that older titles had were no where to be seen (except you Gimighoul Coins!)

So after 80+ hours clocked it is safe to say that I had a great time with Pokemon Scarlet and am looking forward to Gen 10!

But again play this on Nintendo Switch 2 as that is the best way to play and is a shame that it released the way it did at launch.

8/10
Didnt play this until after the Switch2 released. What an amazing game. If this released on S2 hardware the reviews would be very different. One of the best Pokémon experiences I have had.

Good game, I think the main story of the three paths could have been denser, especially the main one about the Pokémon League. The post-game story and the DLCs are good, I think they could have gone even deeper into the topic of paradoxes, like including them more in the main story, not just in the **** addition, they could have “locked” some areas so that it wouldn't be so easy to break the game. Something I'd like to see in the future, if they decide to keep the world open, is for trainers and gym leaders to have flexible teams based on your medals. That way, you could almost decide your path, with certain limitations, of course, such as giving you the freedom to explore the first four areas and go in whatever order you want for the first four gyms, and so on.Finally, improve the graphics. I'm not asking to be able to see the characters' sweat, but I would like to see better-constructed landscapes, with little or no popping. and something they absolutely must focus on in future games is that areas with wild Pokémon should feel more alive, not just with Pokémon walking or flying around, but doing different things, just like in the anime, with Pokémon interacting with the environment, and each Pokémon with its own nature changing depending on your behavior, as well as other trainers. It would be interesting to go along the routes and see NPCs trying to catch a Pokémon or training.
Needs more polish.......................................................... Why are we releasing it with so many bugs and a general lack of quality compared to the other Pokémon games?
Nice gameeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeerrrrrrrrrrrrrrrr
You know what. This is a good game overall just having some technical problems. I suggest getting the Switch 2 version as it’s a much better experience!

This is the Generation 9 Pokemon game, it takes place in Paldea Region which is based on Spain. It's a fun game but it's lacking in content and it's not polished or finished. It is not very professionally made. Some of the pokemon designs are really ugly. I still thought it was fun though and probably worth like $20 honestly lol.

Pokemon Scarlet is the first time in a LONG time that I completed a Pokemon game and the this is the first Pokemon game I have finished the Pokédex!

Before anything else I must say I did play this on the Nintendo Switch 2 and OH MAN does it make a difference! And I am only writing this review now as this was the first time I played it and the experience was so good! 60fps, shiny hunting made a lot easier and the time consuming nonsense that older titles had were no where to be seen (except you Gimighoul Coins!)

So after 80+ hours clocked it is safe to say that I had a great time with Pokemon Scarlet and am looking forward to Gen 10!

But again play this on Nintendo Switch 2 as that is the best way to play and is a shame that it released the way it did at launch.

8/10
